features: TL-UL CSR read/write access for 19-register map
Reset behavior and default CSR values
Format FIFO write path via FDATA
Host RX FIFO read path via RDATA
Target TX FIFO write path via TXDATA
Target ACQ FIFO read path via ACQDATA
Arbitration loss handling in multi-master scenarios
Concurrent host/target enable interaction and priority resolution

directed_tests.name: reset_smoke_csr_defaults
directed_tests.intent: Apply reset, verify all readable CSRs return documented reset/default values, writable CSRs accept writes, reserved bits read as zero, and TL-UL read/write handshakes complete cleanly.

directed_tests.name: csr_rw_ro_wo_w1c_sanity
directed_tests.intent: Exercise each CSR access type: RW readback, RO immutability, WO write acceptance with no meaningful readback, and W1C clearing of INTR_STATE bits.

directed_tests.name: fifo_reset_and_status_flags
directed_tests.intent: Write FIFO_CTRL reset controls, confirm FIFO_STATUS and STATUS reflect empty/full transitions, and verify no stale data remains after reset.

directed_tests.name: host_mode_single_byte_write
directed_tests.intent: Enable host mode, program timing CSRs, enqueue a simple format command, and verify SCL/SDA open-drain activity and completion status for a minimal write transaction.

directed_tests.name: host_mode_readback_path
directed_tests.intent: Perform a host read transaction and confirm received data appears in RDATA/RX FIFO path with expected status and interrupt behavior.

directed_tests.name: target_mode_receive_and_acquire
directed_tests.intent: Enable target mode, match TARGET_ID, drive an external host transaction, and verify received bytes are captured in ACQDATA with correct status updates.

directed_tests.name: target_mode_transmit_path
directed_tests.intent: Preload TXDATA, trigger a target read response, and verify the controller sources data correctly on SDA while maintaining open-drain semantics.

directed_tests.name: interrupt_state_enable_test
directed_tests.intent: Induce representative events, verify INTR_STATE bits set, mask/unmask with INTR_ENABLE, inject via INTR_TEST, and clear with W1C writes.

directed_tests.name: line_loopback_functional
directed_tests.intent: Enable CTRL.line_loopback and confirm internal routing of SCL/SDA allows self-contained transaction progress without external bus drivers.

directed_tests.name: arbitration_loss_scenario
directed_tests.intent: Create a multi-master contention case on SDA, verify arbitration loss detection, bus release behavior, and recovery to idle.

directed_tests.name: timeout_configuration_and_expiry
directed_tests.intent: Program HOST_TIMEOUT_CTRL to a short threshold, stall bus progress, and verify timeout status/interrupt behavior and safe recovery.

directed_tests.name: ovrd_debug_override
directed_tests.intent: Exercise OVRD to force bus line states, confirm override precedence over normal FSM drive, and ensure outputs remain open-drain compliant.

random_tests.name: tlul_csr_fuzz_smoke
random_tests.intent: Randomized TL-UL read/write sequences over all implemented CSRs with scoreboarding for access policy, reset persistence, and reserved-bit masking.

random_tests.name: host_fsm_random_transactions
random_tests.intent: Constrained-random host-mode command generation covering start/stop/read/write/restart patterns, varying timing CSRs and FIFO depths to hit host FSM branches.

random_tests.name: target_fsm_random_transactions
random_tests.intent: Constrained-random target-mode stimulus with randomized address matches, read/write directions, and TX/ACQ FIFO interactions to exercise target FSM branches.

random_tests.name: mixed_role_contention_random
random_tests.intent: Randomly enable host and target together, vary external bus activity, and check arbitration, bus ownership, and no illegal simultaneous drive on SCL/SDA.

random_tests.name: interrupt_and_timeout_random
random_tests.intent: Randomly toggle interrupt enables, inject test interrupts, and vary timeout settings while transactions are in flight to validate event reporting robustness.

risk_areas.reason: Most likely source of functional bugs due to multi-step I2C protocol timing, FIFO consumption, and interaction with programmable timing CSRs.
Dual-role operation increases state complexity; target-mode data capture and response paths are easy to mis-handle under concurrent bus activity.
Protocol corner case with high defect risk; incorrect release/retry behavior can deadlock the bus or corrupt transactions.

risk_areas.area: FIFO status coherency and reset handling
risk_areas.reason: Four 64-entry FIFOs plus status flags create risk of off-by-one, stale-data, and reset synchronization issues.
Interrupt bugs are common and can be validated quickly with directed and randomized CSR tests.
Electrical protocol compliance depends on never driving high and correctly releasing lines under all modes.
Programmable timing parameters may expose arithmetic or boundary issues, especially at extreme values.
Debug features often bypass normal control flow and can hide latent muxing bugs, but are lower risk than core protocol paths.
